title: Intronic Signaling Pathway System (ISPS): Extended Scientific Documentation
author: Lopez Neblina, Fernando
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This document pre-registers the Intronic Signaling Pathway System (ISPS), a theoretical model proposing that RNA introns, especially in lariat form, retain regulatory capacity by interacting with transcription factors such as NFκB1. This timestamp serves as proof of authorship and priority.
steps_raw: This document pre-registers the Intronic Signaling Pathway System (ISPS), a theoretical model proposing that RNA introns, especially in lariat form, retain regulatory capacity by interacting with transcription factors such as NFκB1.
